turns.text: Thanks for calling Sears Home Services — what appliance can I help you with today?
My dryer is acting up and I want to get ahead of it.
Happy to help — what's the brand and model?
Thanks. What's it doing — any error codes or unusual sounds?
It shows error code E1, and there's a high-pitched squealing when the drum turns.
Got it — an E1 code plus a squeal when the drum turns. When did this begin?

On this Whirlpool, E1 points to a thermistor or sensor fault, and a squeal when the drum turns usually means a worn idler pulley or drum glide. Unplug the dryer, then feel whether the drum turns freely by hand — a rough or noisy spin confirms the pulley. The E1 and the squeal together are worth a technician, but the unplug-and-spin check tells us how urgent it is.
Understood, I'll unplug it and take a look.
Great — if the drum feels rough or the E1 stays after a power cycle, call back and we'll schedule a technician for the pulley and sensor.

symptoms.description: error code E1 with a squealing noise when the drum turns
symptoms.sound: a high-pitched squealing when the drum turns

steps_given: Turn the drum by hand to feel whether it spins freely or roughly.
Power-cycle to see whether the E1 code clears.
